abstract.text: Understand, use, and preview the next-generation build system for Package Manager.

metadata.title: [6.3] Preview the Swift Build System Integration

inlineContent.text: Swift Package Manager is previewing a new build system
as a replacement for the current native build system. This document outlines the current state of the preview, key differences, and known issues.
the new build system in your project and
For more information about the migration to Swift Build, see
The Swift Build integration follows these phases:
- Address remaining gaps and platform issues
- Transition to Swift Build as default

content.text: How to use the Swift Build build system

inlineContent.text: To participate in the preview, run your Swift Package Manager commands with the

The Swift Build build system will generate an error on Windows until static libraries are supported. See
, SwiftPM now consistently applies the same set of resource rules as xcodebuild when a package contains resource types like asset catalogs, storyboards, Metal sources, etc.

abstract.text: Build Swift packages with an existing continuous integration setup and prepare apps that consume package dependencies within an existing CI pipeline.
Include C language code as a target in your Swift package.
Bundle executable or shareable code into a standalone Swift package.
Create aliased names for modules to avoid collisions between targets in your package or its dependencies.
